twin_relationship: Refined preview/cleanup variant of scene 10 (Crystal Cutter Tutorial). Same R28+R29 chained-solver architecture; cleaner naming; no production rendering scaffolding; explicit author 'Press Play' annotation.
purpose: The minimal-Scene-Nodes-footprint variant of the Crystal Cutter family. Same procedural faceted crystal generation as scene 10, but with cleaner naming ('Corners Cuts' / 'Split Cuts' instead of 'Tip' / 'Horizontal'), refined motion control (3 nulls per cutter + 2 Random Fields), and minimal Scene Nodes presence (10 nodes total, 5 per memory holder).
visual_result: Confirmed by Spenser pressing Play: 'tons of variable linear cuts on the low poly crystal'. Boolean cuts produce flat planar faces; per-frame randomization gives variable angles per cut.

spenser_hypotheses_confirmed.scene_nodes_minimal: Yes — only 10 Scene Nodes graph nodes total (2× 5-node memory@ containers).
spenser_hypotheses_confirmed.memory_node_frame_per_frame: Yes — memory@ in each Store-for-next-Frame holds prev-frame state; Connect Instance reflects it; Legacy Boole subtracts moving cutter; result becomes next memory.
spenser_hypotheses_confirmed.tons_of_procedural_cuts: Yes — 100 frame timeline at FPS=200 produces hundreds of accumulated Boolean A∖B subtractions.

spenser_play_observation: tons of variable linear cuts on the low poly crystal

annotations.text: Pres Play to see it in action!
annotations.significance: FIRST-PARTY confirmation of gotcha #59 — author explicitly tells users to PLAY rather than scrub. Validates the architectural constraint.

hosts.role: memory@ container, identical to scenes 09 and 10

om_classic_generators.name: Cut Cube 0 (Split Cuts)
om_classic_generators.role: ULTRA-THIN PLANE cutter for horizontal cuts (cube-as-plane)

om_classic_generators.name: Cylinder
om_classic_generators.role: input seed mesh for first solver

om_classic_generators.name: Continouse Spin / Rotate Angle / Move in and out (×3 nulls)
om_classic_generators.role: 3-DOF compound motion control for Corners Cuts cutter

om_classic_generators.name: Move top to Bottom / Random Spin / Rotate Angle (×3 nulls)
om_classic_generators.role: 3-DOF compound motion control for Split Cuts cutter

om_classic_generators.name: Angle (×2 Random Fields)
om_classic_generators.role: randomizes Rotate Angle null per frame

om_classic_generators.name: In and Out / Spin (×2 Random Fields)
om_classic_generators.role: randomizes secondary motion null per frame

om_classic_generators.name: Init State (×2 Connects)
om_classic_generators.role: frame-0 seed; second solver's Init is a Connect Instance pointing at first solver's Solved State (chaining)

om_classic_generators.name: Solved State Corner Cut / Solved State Splits (Connects)
om_classic_generators.role: per-frame solver wraps containing Legacy Boole

om_classic_generators.name: Cut Cube Instance + State from previous Frame (Connect Instances)
om_classic_generators.role: OM-level cross-references for cutter and state

deltas_vs_scene_10: Names cleaner: 'Corners Cuts' vs 'Tip Cutting'; 'Split Cuts' vs 'Horizontal Cutting'
3 motion nulls per cutter (vs 2) — more DOF for cutter motion
2 Random Fields per stack (vs 1) — independent randomization for independent motion axes
Plane cutter is now 600×600×1 (vs 300×300×0.5) — even thinner aspect ratio
FPS 200 (vs 250); MAX 100 (vs 130) — fewer iterations but same architecture
NOTE annotation present (vs absent in scene 10)
'Cut Cube 0' naming hints at extensibility (room for Cut Cube 1, 2, ...)
what_is_clever_additional: Annotation tag with PLAY directive — author gave explicit operational guidance. METHODOLOGY: read annotations FIRST.
Three-null motion control gives 3 DOF per cutter — richer cut patterns than scene 10's 2-null setup.
Two-field randomization decorrelates motion axes — more organic-looking cut distributions.
'Cut Cube 0' naming hints at extensibility — recipe should expose multi-cutter support.
Minimal Scene Nodes footprint (10 nodes total) — proves R28+R29's leverage.

minimal_reproducible_subgraph.name: minimum_scene_nodes_footprint
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.purpose: Achieve a complex procedural simulation with the SMALLEST possible Scene Nodes graph footprint. memory@ ONLY for state; everything else in classic OM.
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.value_proposition: Maximum architectural leverage per Scene Nodes graph node. Easier to author for artists fluent in classic C4D; easier to debug (every wire visible in OM); easier to share (recipes become 'OM templates with stock memory@ wrapper').
secondary_recipes: R35_compound_motion_null_hierarchy — Continuous Spin + Rotate Angle + Move in/out as reusable compound motion pattern
R33_cube_as_plane_cutter — extreme-aspect-ratio Cube as a closed-volume Boolean B that functions as a plane
R36_decorrelated_random_fields — multiple Random Fields per cutter for independent motion axis randomization
blockers_to_recipe_implementation: Need to formalize 'minimum-Scene-Nodes-footprint' as a recipe style alongside 'maximum-Scene-Nodes-power' style
Compound motion null hierarchy creation in MCP needs API surface
Recipe meta-language for declaring 'add cutter N' extensibility (per Cut Cube 0 naming hint)

lessons_for_cinema4d_mcp: Minimum-footprint Scene Nodes is a recipe style worth distinguishing from maximum-power style.
READ ANNOTATIONS FIRST — author guidance trumps automated probing. Update methodology checklist.
Naming conventions hint at extensibility ('Cut Cube 0' = room for 1, 2, 3). Recipes should expose these.
Motion-null hierarchies are recipe-able patterns (compound motion via stacked nulls).
Multiple decorrelated Random Fields per cutter produce more organic distributions than single field driving everything.
